title: hemerocallis flava var. yezoensis
intro: Hemerocallis flava var. yezoensis, also known as the golden daylily, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the genus Hemerocallis. This plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Japan, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The golden daylily has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as fever, sore throats, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, including colds and flu, and to promote overall well-being.
